title: Wave of Israeli Strikes Kills at Least Four Palestinians Across Gaza — 14-Year-Old Dies of Earlier Injuries
detail: A new wave of Israeli military strikes killed at least four Palestinians across Gaza on April 26, 2026, with Al Jazeera, Middle East Monitor, and Times of Israel documenting multiple simultaneous attack vectors: (1) An air attack killed one person at Al-Mughraqa in central Gaza; (2) An Israeli drone struck a motorcycle near the Kuwait Roundabout on Salah al-Din Street in Gaza City, killing two Palestinians — three additional Palestinians were subsequently shot by a quadcopter drone in the same area; (3) A 40-year-old woman was shot dead by Israeli forces in Khan Younis; (4) Israeli helicopter gunfire and artillery targeted eastern areas of Al-Bureij refugee camp; (5) Israeli artillery shelled Al-Tuffah neighborhood east of Gaza City; (6) Israeli naval boats fired shells at the Gaza coast. A 14-year-old teenager died from injuries suffered in an earlier Israeli strike in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood. An IDF soldier was separately seriously injured in a non-combat operational accident in Gaza. Al Jazeera's ceasefire analysis published the same day confirmed the cumulative post-October 2025 ceasefire Palestinian death toll has now exceeded 800.

media.caption: Israeli attacks kill at least four Palestinians in Gaza despite nominal ceasefire, April 26, 2026

title: Fatah Claims 'Sweeping Victory' in 2026 Palestinian Local Elections; Deir al-Balah Turnout 22.7%
detail: Fatah claimed a 'sweeping victory' in the April 25, 2026 Palestinian municipal elections as results were announced on April 26. Fatah stated it 'formed 197 municipal and village councils by consensus,' winning the majority of local councils including Jenin. West Bank final turnout reached approximately 53.44%; Deir al-Balah (Gaza) turnout was 22.7% — low but notable given 20 years without elections and ongoing conflict. Critics noted democratic shortcomings: in cities like Nablus and Ramallah, only a single Fatah-affiliated candidate list was submitted, resulting in automatic victories without a competitive vote. Hamas was formally barred from running, with candidates required to recognize Israel and support the two-state solution. The Jerusalem Post reported the results as a significant political win for Mahmoud Abbas's Fatah, though analysts cautioned that the elections' limited scope and Hamas's exclusion undermined claims of broad democratic legitimacy.

media.caption: Fatah claims sweeping victory in 2026 Palestinian local elections; results announced April 26

title: 800+ Palestinians Killed Since October 2025 Ceasefire; Gaza Deadlock Continues Post-Regional Ceasefires
detail: Al Jazeera published a major analysis on April 26, 2026 confirming that approximately 800 Palestinians have been killed since the October 2025 ceasefire implementation — crossing a grim threshold as the Gaza deadlock deepens despite two regional ceasefires (US-Iran since April 8; Israel-Lebanon extended three weeks at White House talks). Key findings: 2,400+ documented ceasefire violations per Gaza's Government Media Office; aid trucks entering Gaza averaged only 150–190 per day against the 600/day agreed in ceasefire terms; Israel controls approximately 60% of the Gaza enclave via its 'Yellow Line' partition system in eastern Gaza; the core ceasefire dispute remains Hamas's refusal to disarm versus Israel's refusal to complete military withdrawal; analysts assess a prolonged stalemate as the most likely near-term outcome. The analysis notes that while two of the three major regional fronts have achieved ceasefires, the Gaza core conflict — with its unresolved questions of Hamas governance, Palestinian statehood, and post-war administration under the US-backed Board of Peace — remains far from resolution.

media.caption: Analysis of Gaza ceasefire deadlock: 800+ Palestinians killed since October 2025 ceasefire, April 26, 2026
